title: Design System: Hustad Residential Tablet Platform

display.purpose: Screen titles — one per screen, top of content area. Playfair Display earns trust on sight.

headline.purpose: Section headings within a screen. Used sparingly; one focused screen rarely needs one.

body.purpose: Descriptive copy, compliance text, descriptions. 65–75ch on prose screens.

label.purpose: Form labels, list labels, button text. Sentence case — never all-caps.

mono.purpose: Phase badges (PHASE A / PHASE B), status codes, reference IDs.

shadows.name: card-ambient
shadows.purpose: Default card lift. Never decorative — only with a 1px navy-border companion.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Interactive card hover state. Communicates selectability.

shadows.name: elevated
shadows.purpose: Modal and popover elevation. Structural, not ambient.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Input and interactive element focus ring. Sky-blue tinted, low opacity.

motion.name: screen-enter
motion.purpose: Screen transition on route change. The only page-level animation.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Button and interactive element state changes. Fast — users are in a task flow.

motion.name: progress-fill
motion.purpose: Progress bar fill — longer duration conveys meaningful advancement.

breakpoints.name: iPad Air portrait (primary)
breakpoints.purpose: The only target viewport. No responsive design below this breakpoint in core screens.

components.name: Primary Button (Navy)
components.description: Main forward-action button. Full-width, used for the primary progression action on each screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 16px 24px; background: #0f1d35; color: #E8EDF8; border: none; border-radius: 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms linear; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1a2d4f; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(74,143,212,0.15); }

components.name: Teal CTA Button
components.description: Primary submission / commitment action. Use when the action is a meaningful commitment (save, submit, confirm).
components.css: .ds-btn-teal { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 16px 24px; background: #2a8a82; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms linear; } .ds-btn-teal:hover { background: #3aada3; } .ds-btn-teal:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-teal:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(42,138,130,0.25); }

components.name: Amber Signature Button
components.description: Reserved for signature capture and agreement completion screens. The warm amber signals the weight of the action.
components.css: .ds-btn-amber { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 16px 24px; background: #c8923a; color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms linear; } .ds-btn-amber:hover { background: #e0a94a; } .ds-btn-amber:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-amber:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(200,146,58,0.25); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Back navigation and secondary choices. Transparent with a hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 16px 24px; background: transparent; color: #E8EDF8; border: 1px solid #1e3050; border-radius: 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms linear; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #2d4060; background: rgba(30,48,80,0.3); } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(74,143,212,0.15); }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Default content card. 1px navy border + ambient shadow + 16px radius.
components.html:
Job Address

1234 Elm Street, Madison WI
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0d1525; border: 1px solid #1e3050; border-radius: 16px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 2px 16px rgba(15,29,53,0.10), 0 1px 3px rgba(15,29,53,0.06); transition: box-shadow 150ms linear; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(15,29,53,0.16), 0 2px 8px rgba(15,29,53,0.10); } .ds-card-label { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #8BA5C5; margin: 0 0 4px 0; letter-spacing: 0.01em; } .ds-card-value { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; color: #F3F4F6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Form input — documentation fields. Navy-surface background with navy border.
components.css: .ds-field-wrapper { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-label { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #E8EDF8; letter-spacing: 0.01em; } .ds-input { background: #0d1525; border: 1px solid #1e3050; border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px 16px; color: #E8EDF8; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; outline: none; transition: all 150ms linear; box-sizing: border-box; width: 100%; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #567090; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #2563ba; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(74,143,212,0.15); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Screen Footer (Glass Chrome)
components.description: Persistent footer for navigation actions. The only glassmorphism element in the system — backdrop-blur over the dark content.
components.css: .ds-screen-footer { flex-shrink: 0; display: flex; gap: 12px; padding: 20px 32px; border-top: 1px solid #1e3050; background: color-mix(in srgb, #0d1525 80%, transparent); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(4px); } .ds-footer-btn { flex: 1; }

components.name: Phase Badge
components.description: Screen phase indicator. DM Mono, uppercase, pill-shaped. Used in screen header to orient the rep in the flow.
components.css: .ds-phase-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'DM Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; } .ds-phase-a { background: rgba(26,45,79,0.8); color: #8BA5C5; } .ds-phase-b { background: rgba(42,138,130,0.15); color: #3aada3; }

components.name: Screen Title
components.description: Top-of-screen heading. Playfair Display — the rep's first visual anchor on each step.
components.html:
Phase A

Document the Damage

Photograph each affected area with the compliance checklist visible.
components.css: .ds-screen-header { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-screen-title { margin: 0; font-family: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 4vw, 2rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.15; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #E8EDF8; text-wrap: balance; } .ds-screen-subtitle { margin: 0; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.0125rem; font-weight: 300; line-height: 1.5; color: #8BA5C5; }

narrative.overview:
The Hustad Tablet Platform is a professional instrument, not a consumer app. It is a dark, precise, high-craft surface designed for field reps presenting documented storm damage findings to homeowners on an iPad Air in portrait orientation. The interface projects expert authority — the kind of tool that earns trust on sight, before the rep has said a word.

The palette is built around near-void blacks and layered dark navy surfaces, with Playfair Display headings that carry editorial weight against the dark. Teal punctuates primary actions; amber signals attention states; sky-blue carries interactive highlights and focus rings. Glassmorphism appears as purposeful chrome — frosted backdrop over dark surfaces for screen footers and elevated panels — never as decoration or identity.

This system explicitly rejects the startup dark-mode aesthetic, consumer insurance app friendliness, and generic enterprise field-service density. It should feel like the screen version of a forensic assessment brief — precise, weighted, unapologetic.
keyCharacteristics: Near-void background (#06090F) with three distinct dark navy depth levels
Playfair Display for screen titles and presentation headings; DM Sans for all functional UI text
Glassmorphism is a purposeful architectural element for footer chrome and modals only
Teal (#2a8a82) as primary action accent; amber (#c8923a) as signature/completion signal
Fixed iPad Air portrait viewport — no fluid typography, no responsive breakpoints in the core flow

rules.name: The One Surface Rule
rules.body: The four dark navy surface colors (void/surface/elevated/subtle) are the only background fills for standard screens. No custom backgrounds on cards or sections.

rules.name: The Two-Accent Rule
rules.body: Teal carries forward-commitment actions. Amber carries signature and completion moments. Blue-action carries focus and interactive affordance only. Nothing else carries accent color.

rules.name: The Contrast Floor
rules.body: Body text must sustain ≥4.5:1 contrast on all surfaces. Ink-muted (#567090) is forbidden as body text color on any dark surface.

rules.name: The Serif-Only Rule
rules.body: Playfair Display is for screen titles and section headings only. Buttons, labels, chips, form fields, and table data use DM Sans.

rules.name: The Inter-Only Rule
rules.body: On CRM and pipeline data surfaces, use Inter with tabular figures. Playfair Display does not appear on data-dense screens.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: All typography uses fixed rem values. Only .screen-title uses clamp(). No other type element uses fluid sizing.

rules.name: The Glass Protocol
rules.body: Glassmorphism is permitted only on the screen footer (backdrop-blur-sm + 80% surface opacity) and modal overlays. Forbidden everywhere else.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Cards at rest carry a 1px navy-border and card-ambient shadow. Do not pair a wide shadow (blur ≥16px) with a solid border at rest — that is the ghost-card pattern.

dos: Do use Playfair Display for screen titles and section headings only.
Do use the four-layer dark surface system for all backgrounds.
Do keep teal for forward-commitment actions and amber for signature moments.
Do use the glass chrome pattern exclusively on the screen footer and modal overlays.
Do maintain ≥44px tap targets on every interactive element.
Do use Inter with tabular figures on RepCommandCenter, PipelineLeads, and CenterPointJobs.
Do add transition: all 150ms and active:scale-[0.98] on all button elements.
Do write screen titles with text-wrap: balance for even line breaks.
donts: Don't use glassmorphism on cards, list items, section containers, or scrolling content elements.
Don't pair a border: 1px solid with a wide box-shadow (blur ≥16px) on the same element at rest.
Don't use violet, purple, or neon accent colors — the palette is navy, teal, and amber only.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Emphasis is through weight and size contrast.
Don't use Playfair Display in buttons, labels, chips, table cells, or form controls.
Don't add fluid clamp() typography beyond .screen-title.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored side accent.
Don't make the interface look like a startup AI tool — no gradient mesh backgrounds, no particle effects.
Don't use generic enterprise mobile patterns (large icon-grid touch targets, white-background card grids).
Don't introduce colors outside the defined palette for new screens.
